description: Add a local embedded Hindsight companion that improves memory recall and extraction while keeping OpenCode's local JSON memory store authoritative.

userStories.title: Add Hindsight config schema and defaults
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want explicit Hindsight config so that rollout, fallback, and resource budgets are controlled by validated settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Add `memory.hindsight` config in `packages/opencode/src/config/config.ts` with `enabled`, `mode`, `extract`, `recall`, `backfill`, `workspace_scope`, `bank_prefix`, `startup_timeout_ms`, `query_timeout_ms`, `retain_limit`, `recall_limit`, `observation_limit`, `context_max_items`, `context_max_tokens`, and `log_level`
Default values are `enabled: false`, `mode: "embedded"`, `extract: true`, `recall: true`, `backfill: true`, `workspace_scope: "worktree"`, `context_max_items: 6`, and `context_max_tokens: 1200`
Invalid Hindsight config fails during parsing instead of later runtime use
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added a validated `memory.hindsight` config schema with phase-1 defaults and config tests covering default application plus invalid-mode rejection.

userStories.title: Add embedded Hindsight service lifecycle
userStories.description: As a user, I want OpenCode to start and reuse a local embedded Hindsight service so that semantic retrieval works without a cloud dependency.
acceptanceCriteria: Add workspace-scoped Hindsight service module under `packages/opencode/src/memory/hindsight/`
Use `@vectorize-io/hindsight-all` as the default embedded path and bind only to loopback
Service starts lazily, reuses one handle per worktree, and supports health, timeout, degraded, and shutdown behavior
Startup or health failure falls back without crashing memory flows
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Hindsight client wrapper
userStories.description: As a developer, I want one client wrapper around Hindsight SDK calls so that recall, retain, and backfill share the same timeout and error handling.
acceptanceCriteria: Add client wrapper module that connects through the embedded service using `@vectorize-io/hindsight-client`
Expose thin wrappers for retain, batch retain, recall, and document or operation inspection calls needed by backfill
Apply shared timeout and non-fatal error handling in the wrapper instead of scattering SDK calls across engine code
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add worktree bank identity and stable document mapping
userStories.description: As a developer, I want deterministic bank and document mapping so that retain, recall, and backfill stay idempotent.
acceptanceCriteria: Add deterministic worktree hash and bank id helpers such as `opencode:${worktreeHash}`
Generate stable `mem:`, `sess:`, and `obs:` document ids plus string-only metadata and normalized tags
Resolve recall hits by preferring reversible `document_id` parsing and then `metadata.memory_id`, while rejecting cross-worktree hits
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add retain wrapper for authoritative memory documents
userStories.description: As a developer, I want local memory records normalized into Hindsight retain calls so that the companion bank mirrors authoritative memory content safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Add retain or upsert wrapper that maps one local memory record to one Hindsight document in the worktree bank
Use stable `mem:` document ids, string-only metadata, normalized tags, and replace-style idempotent updates
Retain failures are logged and returned as non-fatal results instead of blocking local memory writes
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Hindsight recall query and resolve pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ranked Hindsight hits resolved back to local memory ids so that downstream recall logic only sees authoritative candidates.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Hindsight recall wrapper with timeout handling and ranked result parsing
Resolve hits into local memory ids while dropping unresolved, stale, cross-worktree, `sess:`, and `obs:` hits from direct injection
Preserve both raw relevance score and ranked order on resolved candidates for downstream selection
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Integrate Hindsight ranking into memory recall
userStories.description: As a user, I want recall to use Hindsight-ranked authoritative candidates so that the assistant selects more relevant existing memories.
acceptanceCriteria: Update `packages/opencode/src/memory/engine/recall.ts` to query Hindsight when `memory.hindsight.enabled` and `memory.hindsight.recall` are true
Pass resolved authoritative local memory candidates plus raw score and rank into `memory-recall` for final selection
Fallback to the current recall path on startup failure, timeout, or unusable Hindsight results
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Preserve local authority lifecycle boundaries
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want Hindsight integration to leave lifecycle ownership on local memory so that retrieval improves without rewriting storage, inject, hit tracking, or decay rules.
acceptanceCriteria: Keep `packages/opencode/src/memory/storage.ts` as the authoritative store in phase 1
Keep `inject`, `hit-tracker`, and decay paths operating on local authoritative memory records instead of raw Hindsight responses
Enable Hindsight recall and extract participation without changing local lifecycle field ownership such as `status`, `score`, `useCount`, or `hitCount`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add a Hindsight-aware extractor variant
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a dedicated Hindsight-aware extractor path so that extract integration stays aligned with the existing memory-extractor contract.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a dedicated Hindsight-aware extractor variant instead of bolting unbounded context onto the existing extractor prompt
Keep the variant compatible with current authoritative memory create or update outputs
If Hindsight context is unavailable, the extractor path can still run without failing memory extraction
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add extractor context budget enforcement
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want explicit item and token budgets for Hindsight extractor context so that extraction quality stays bounded.
acceptanceCriteria: Enforce both `context_max_items` and `context_max_tokens` budgets in the Hindsight-aware extractor path
Use defaults of `6` items and `1200` tokens when no override is provided
Discard or trim excess Hindsight context before prompt construction instead of exceeding the configured budget
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Retain session slices for extraction context
userStories.description: As a developer, I want session slices retained with stable ids so that extraction can query semantically related local context.
acceptanceCriteria: Retain current session slices with stable `sess:` document ids before or alongside extraction
Use the worktree bank, string-only metadata, and normalized tags for session-slice retention
Retention failure stays non-fatal to the extract flow
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Inject Hindsight context into extraction
userStories.description: As a user, I want extraction to see semantically related context from retained sessions so that new memories and updates are more accurate.
acceptanceCriteria: Query Hindsight for related documents or observations and pass a bounded `Hindsight context` section into the Hindsight-aware extractor
Local memory writes still go through authoritative `Memory.create()` and `Memory.update()` paths
Extractor falls back cleanly when Hindsight returns no usable context
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Retain authoritative memory writes after create and update
userStories.description: As a developer, I want changed local memories upserted into Hindsight so that recall stays aligned with the authoritative store over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Hook memory create and update flows to upsert the affected authoritative memory document into Hindsight
Repeated updates reuse the same stable `mem:` document id instead of creating duplicates
Retain failure never blocks the local authoritative memory write path
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Hindsight sidecar state persistence
userStories.description: As a developer, I want structured per-worktree Hindsight state stored outside the authority file so that backfill can resume safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Add sidecar state helpers that read and write a per-worktree `hindsight.json` file next to local memory data
Persist bank identity, scope, timestamps, and structured backfill state including status, cursor, counts, operation ids, and failure samples
Do not change the shape of the existing authoritative local memory file to store Hindsight state
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add resumable backfill runner for local memories
userStories.description: As an existing user, I want current local memories imported into Hindsight so that semantic recall is useful immediately after enablement.
acceptanceCriteria: Add backfill runner that reads local memories and imports them through Hindsight retain APIs using stable `mem:` document ids
Persist cursor, progress, and failures in the Hindsight sidecar while using replace-style idempotent upserts
Re-running backfill resumes safely and does not create duplicate authoritative mappings
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Auto-start and resume backfill on enable
userStories.description: As a user, I want backfill to begin automatically when Hindsight is enabled so that the companion becomes useful without extra manual setup.
acceptanceCriteria: Enabling Hindsight starts backfill automatically unless a config escape hatch disables auto-start for testing or recovery
Interrupted backfill resumes from saved sidecar state instead of starting over blindly
Disabling Hindsight after backfill does not break local memory reads or prompt injection
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add observability and degraded-mode behavior
userStories.description: As a developer, I want structured logs and safe degraded behavior so that rollout issues are visible without breaking sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: Add structured logs for service startup, health, retain, recall query, extractor assist, stale drops, fallback, and backfill progress
Mark Hindsight degraded after startup failure and keep local memory create, update, and injection flows running
Include durations, counts, and fallback reasons without logging more content than current memory logs allow
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add a read-only Hindsight inspect command
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a minimal status command so that I can inspect Hindsight service health and backfill state during rollout.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a minimal internal read-only command or entrypoint that reports service health, bank identity, scope, and backfill state
The inspect command does not mutate Hindsight data or local memory state
Output is sufficient to diagnose whether enablement, service startup, and backfill are healthy
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add mapping and recall regression coverage
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want targeted regression coverage for config, mapping, and recall resolution so that future changes do not silently break recall behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Add unit coverage for config parsing, bank id generation, document id generation, metadata normalization, and resolve rules
Add regression coverage for recall fallback, stale-hit dropping, and score or rank propagation into `memory-recall`
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add extract and backfill regression coverage
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want targeted regression coverage for extract integration and backfill recovery so that future changes do not silently break the companion flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Add regression coverage for Hindsight-aware extraction, context budget enforcement, and authoritative memory write behavior
Add regression coverage for backfill resume, idempotent rerun, and degraded behavior when the embedded service is unavailable
Keep any real embedded-daemon smoke path opt-in for CI or local verification instead of making it a hard default test requirement
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
